sections.paragraph: plur. תְּאֵנִים f., a fig tree, Gen 3:7 (where the Indian fig or Musa paradisiaca, Germ. Paradiesfeigenbaum, with large leaves, is apparently meant), Num 13:23 20:5 Deu 8:8, etc., also a fig, the fruit, 2Ki 20:7. (The etymology is unknown, for it can neither be suitably derived from the root אוּן, nor from תָּאַן; Arab. قأن Conj. III.). “To sit under one’s vine and under one’s fig tree” is said of those who lead a tranquil and happy life, 1Ki 5:5 Zec 3:10 Mic 4:4. See Celsii Hierobot. t. ii. p. 368-399.
